applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel fusion (ReLU + LeakyReLU + GELU + Sigmoid + BiasAdd)
applied_optimizations.description: Fused all elementwise activations and bias addition into a single Triton kernel. Used manual tanh implementation with tl.math.exp (tl.libdevice unavailable on ROCm). BLOCK_SIZE=256 aligned with AMD wavefront size of 64.

attempted_but_failed.name: Previous attempt with tl.libdevice.tanh and tl.libdevice.exp
attempted_but_failed.reason: tl.libdevice.* functions are unavailable on ROCm Triton
attempted_but_failed.error: Compilation error at line 54: tl.libdevice.tanh is not available

notes: Successfully fixed ROCm-incompatible code: replaced tl.libdevice.tanh with manual implementation using tl.math.exp, replaced tl.libdevice.exp with tl.math.exp
Main bottleneck is MIOpen conv3d at 34.3% - cannot optimize further with Triton as conv is handled by MIOpen
Transpose overhead (~35%) is internal to MIOpen's grouped conv implementation
The fused activation kernel is efficient at 6.91ms for all 4 activations + bias
BLOCK_SIZE=256 is aligned with AMD wavefront size of 64
Stage 1 complete: baseline established, profiling done, working Triton kernel implemented
